| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105506776/538563592/2021534627?source=SBD_DPG_EM_FR_040925_Voter-Engagement-Fund_2x30DO-60DC-60DD_D1_S2_T1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOTMwZjljZi02ZTE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i5oFh13wzkg4vpJNm_nlUf-ADQ0Sn2Sd3EAKOntnYFg=)
---
Peter, the Georgia GOP is already enacting their plan to suppress voters in the 2026 elections. They recently announced they’re planning to cancel over **_455,000_** “inactive” voter registrations this summer — one of the largest voter purges in history. Voting is a fundamental right, not a use-it-or-lose-it privilege. On top of that, Republicans under the Gold Dome still want to make it vastly more difficult for Georgians to make their voices heard in our elections. name: AOC
subject: IRS + ICE
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
This is how the billionaire class divides and conquers.
#
The cruel spree to terrorize immigrants screws over _all_ working Americans.
Undocumented people pay BILLIONS into Medicare, Social Security, and programs they are ineligible for.
Hunting them also craters the social safety net.
**This is how the billionaire class divides and conquers**.
In solidarity,
Alexandria
The right actively creates fear to get us to give up in advance and withdraw from public life _without them lifting a finger_.
[Hell no. Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250409.aN3a9q&refcode=em20250409-8568-P_FND_CTR_TWT&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=8568%2E2526796%2EGJRfv8)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250409.aN3a9q&refcode=em20250409-8568-P_FND_CTR_TWT&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=8568%2E2526796%2EGJRfv8)**

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250409?refcode=em20250409-8568-P_FND_CTR_TWT&t=21&refcode2=8568_2526796_GJRfv8&akid=8568%2E2526796%2EGJRfv8)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250409-8568-P_FND_CTR_TWT&t=22&akid=8568%2E2526796%2EGJRfv8)**

---|---|---
Email us: [speak@ocasiocortez.com](mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Chris Pappas
subject: The GOP is already targeting New Hampshire
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
| | | |
---
| | | | |
---
| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T80e239eb-08a7-40b4-b111-d5e63ca11023/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
[](https://default.salsalabs.org/T62ac1ba4-d5ce-43d2-9d7d-807da2ad8493/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
| Peter, I’ve been overwhelmed by all the support we’ve received since launching our Senate campaign less than a week ago. Longtime friends and new supporters have come together in a big way to build a strong foundation for our fight for New Hampshire. Still, we know we have a long fight ahead. Donald Trump is already weighing in on our race. That’s right: Donald Trump, the man attacking the livelihoods of Granite Staters with his irrational tariffs, thinks he can anoint the next Senator from New Hampshire. We’ve created an Emergency Trump Response Fund and are setting a bold goal to raise $100,000 in the next 48 hours before our week one fundraising deadline. It’s a test of strength and it sends a message — to the GOP, to national observers, and to the deep-pocketed donors trying to buy this seat — that we’re ready to compete and win. [Peter, we can only reach this fundraising goal with your support. Will you split $10 between our Emergency Trump Response Fund and Blue Amp Action? Every dollar makes a real difference.](https://default.salsalabs.org/T3ce94e55-5322-4394-b0bb-010d563ca4bd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) Let’s be clear: Republicans are already targeting New Hampshire. We know they’ll be flooding the state with corporate money and building their infrastructure to flip this seat. Donald Trump is trying to hand pick a Republican opponent to run against me. National Republicans know how much is at stake in the Granite State – and so do we. We can’t sit on the sidelines of this fight, not with our families’s futures in jeopardy and our democracy on the line. We have to win. Our state and country are depending on us. [Peter, if you’re with me, will you split a $10 donation today between our campaign and Blue Amp Action to help us reach our goal and show we’re ready to keep New Hampshire’s pivotal Senate seat blue?](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tadf1fba7-aaeb-4327-872e-1574742972c4/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE $10](https://default.salsalabs.org/T1f9b51f0-94c3-4072-a803-b570ad4d1d62/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE $20](https://default.salsalabs.org/T46b993e7-0de3-448a-9324-11b35fd7dca6/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE MORE](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tfbda296c-90a3-4722-a48a-73dcb62ce6d2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
|
---
| | | | | With gratitude, Chris

| **Blue Amp Action PAC**
PO Box 8384, Cincinnati, OH 45208
---
| Having trouble viewing this email? [View it in your web browser](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/loc_em_fr_20250409_pappasgoal_b3_f1_s1_c1_92op_n-t1?wvpId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | |
---
| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0c911fda-7458-4807-a793-eec09ac2cb2f/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._
---
| Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
---
| | [Unsubscribe](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/manage-your-subscription?eId=a6e291b6-76af-4270-89a6-a0f6fbef1448&eType=EmailBlastContent&sId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) or [Manage Your Preferences](https://blueampstrategies.salsalabs.org/manage-your-subscription?eId=a6e291b6-76af-4270-89a6-a0f6fbef1448&eType=EmailBlastContent&sId=368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---

name: Mark Levin Alert
subject: A dire warning on judicial tyranny
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Team Elise, a joint fundraising committee authorized by and composed of Elise for Congress and E-PAC
body:
He called out Justice John Roberts!
| ATTN: Peter
---
Mark Levin just issued a dire warning.
---
America could descend into tyranny unless this is immediately STOPPED...
---
[](http://links.americabrigade.com/s/c/bXq-h6wOzTbhI4FJJ3YTyQr1I3svjkj38giX_OkWVesRPM2N747pzYd4vVr9zIsiWIuG650QleKRLcUnMi4oBQ3_3KKOARCHleLUmNMngayioCidPBSe_wGE3jroicZm5k9LyQ1mVqvAyDsZ5vAUnUG8ChTd3M_HN0EUHFbDo0l2t5A4BaM57c-bdIde1Bu-1_LUjMyyzdhEv6TrnLqfPu1WlZuPQixcO3NK6CES4vYO5F8RZlwxFSFmDWGhr94kxp9XGAIwN96-vtqtDkCTJhdw1Ds_ojblcthd5raI0Tq6VWiiBi5hl_KGZ3ai_vVG9iE8sT-DR7z_unT_IQeX_pFO_cTCI5l3ugv4XZzFBiuvZc_a8qXCjb97PNc_5Sv3Fa59GZZPsBtu1MXx4o2-_rpGEjX7oWPPtdjiLxGgVmGtIBUdC5BaTd_kZtjOba1SHa-OI1SFpkTTpPzemTD8f--2wxF8zgYLAVNzOJSVY_8i9Btk/YdUx9q-_F2e7Wl6h-5zPcX-HRfA77_1A/7)
---

name: California Democratic Party
subject: URGENT: Text Voters in CD-22 and CD-40 Before 3PM About Medicaid
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
cadem.org [cadem.org]
Democrats,
We’ve just learned that Republicans in Congress are moving ahead with a key budget vote TODAY, Wednesday, April 9th —and we need your help to fight back.
This isn’t the final budget, but it’s a critical first step that determines the framework. If it passes, they’ll begin committee negotiations based on extreme MAGA priorities— including massive cuts to Medicaid that would impact more than 15 million Californians . We can't afford to let this slip through.
We’re joining our partners at SEIU for an urgent textbank targeting voters in CD-22 and CD-40 , where Rep. David Valadao and Rep. Young Kim need to hear from their constituents immediately.
We need all hands on deck! Texts must go out before 3pm today. Sign up using the links below.

name: Powered By People
subject: Powered by People is ready to elect leaders who serve ALL Texans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
Help us elect an Attorney General who truly represents ALL Texans.
#
Friend,
Yesterday, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton launched a Senate campaign against Republican incumbent Senator John Cornyn.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250409.dUEgh6&refcode=ema20250409-240063&t=1&akid=240063%2E444211%2EQp1NuE)
Paxton’s Senate bid is a direct challenge to the integrity of Congress. Remember, Paxton was _indicted_ for fraud, _impeached_ for bribery, and he abused his power at the expense of everyday Texans.
We can’t afford to let this corruption take root in our state’s highest offices — not with a corrupt administration already in the White House. **Texas needs _real leadership,_ not more of the same toxic politics that only serve the interests of the elite few.**
That's why we need your help today, friend. The stakes in Texas’ Senate race couldn't be higher. If we’re going to make a real difference, we need to hit the ground running. **We must build the necessary resources to ensure that in 2026, we elect a Senator who truly represents ALL Texans.**
[**Chip in now to help us organize, register, and mobilize voters across Texas — so we can elect the leadership Texas deserves.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20250409.dUEgh6&refcode=ema20250409-240063&t=2&akid=240063%2E444211%2EQp1NuE)

Together,

subject: Too many slaves died so that we could have freedom and the opportunity to succeed.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
body:
I won’t let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood. | | | I won’t let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood. | | | | [](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9f0a5858b3d4be5b584171ecb6f79f2&_e=03POQ4FDn_SSQRZAxQsLcl7wW2-d7jxKkuBcmfWUUysDi7MrBv1A3hpCEhItXiksjJHXf31KLrP3PLh04d9REDQtqxTU_rLWu2mZyx9j5WtAcxkEg8ymt-sLx-ll50TnOnwjMs6KBOxCASsoKVUs6e2nnDgccTHzMduaaSSnqX9e6_hfXfCjQ6nqPg9Ok68QAHWl-vGLRlod9py3apAbIasJTVQXKpPD8XuR8HLTjJQwP7m6lshL4fcEK7rQ34Q1dHBEjDav9guP5axeIdPS_a52H2FKrBq_LhGtYt0iiO7HPhb4qJYkeQVhl-18lUPmjWXQj4snWEtS27zRTHfrxCcsDE6rLY1dSADLfryd9zeR5W7P-UFVOWHNWLMOhv26hBo_j8pyiMh9HkwQlJY2hUM8sot-3Tqeh3JK8alz50rZYelCCasBFJ8KBT4uD3zN)
---
**Slaves did not die in the fields so that we could call ourselves victims now in 2025. **

If they saw the DEI nonsense today they would say to us, **_'Is that what we died for??_ _NO_! We died for you to have an _opportunity_ - _take it!'_**

My father came to this country with nothing more than $1.75 in his pocket. He had next to nothing, but with education, grit, and determination, my father built a life for me and my family here.

_**He never asked for a hand out & he never needed one!**_

Peter - Democrats think minorities can't succeed without DEI. They think that we need their help, their labels, and their permission.

_But I 'm the Lt. Governor of Virginia--_**the former capital of the Confederacy.**__ I didn't get where I am today through victimhood but through **faith, education, and grit.**

Right now, I'm running to become the Governor of Virginia. My Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, spews DEI **nonsense** at every chance she gets. But I believe in the American Dream; _anyone who takes responsibility & works hard can succeed in this country!_ Seriously, my father is a perfect example of this; he didn't take a single handout to find success in America!

**DEI is a cancer._I won 't let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood._ **

**Here 's my call to action, Peter: [donate $18 or more to help me end DEI nonsense and lead with the promise of personal responsibility and hard work!](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9f0a5858b3d4be5b584171ecb6f79f2&_e=03POQ4FDn_SSQRZAxQsLcl7wW2-d7jxKkuBcmfWUUysDi7MrBv1A3hpCEhItXiksjJHXf31KLrP3PLh04d9REDQtqxTU_rLWu2mZyx9j5WtAcxkEg8ymt-sLx-ll50TnOnwjMs6KBOxCASsoKVUs6e2nnDgccTHzMduaaSSnqX9e6_hfXfCjQ6nqPg9Ok68QAHWl-vGLRlod9py3apAbIasJTVQXKpPD8XuR8HLTjJQwP7m6lshL4fcEK7rQ34Q1dHBEjDav9guP5axeIdPS_QJ1x9ZRQawd9a15uIzbcBk4JvEgtZV17-ezlAh8-HL0Iw387GIsJXPvzDMGf4SZWb-fNablQERgNvbyNgFNyYQlXhlnx2qgGf94rN6kTs2y0tqaKrbT3U3NumL4Zd3DeuJWw3CddJNqVgqfO9oWeP4mTD3iFuZpM5r_5MwKgnfZ)**
---
[ DONATE NOW >>](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9f0a5858b3d4be5b584171ecb6f79f2&_e=03POQ4FDn_SSQRZAxQsLcl7wW2-d7jxKkuBcmfWUUysDi7MrBv1A3hpCEhItXiksjJHXf31KLrP3PLh04d9REDQtqxTU_rLWu2mZyx9j5WtAcxkEg8ymt-sLx-ll50TnOnwjMs6KBOxCASsoKVUs6e2nnDgccTHzMduaaSSnqX9e6_hfXfCjQ6nqPg9Ok68QAHWl-vGLRlod9py3apAbIasJTVQXKpPD8XuR8HLTjJQwP7m6lshL4fcEK7rQ34Q1dHBEjDav9guP5axeIdPS_V5L38rKDCBRhWfXa5A-Cy1InZPBd4x7LiJYgdtFCMBvIBrbIInuGXAYN5vSjNN-C2DUjquAX1MM4NOjVkHpitgpGpcDDiLXklcDYhFghZIpH9jIkHchDEE5amB0yX1Lg7M-suYERomA1AMELDQnd6e6Guy-4LUzAee9I-wla57L)
---
I am really just so grateful to have you on my team in this fight. Together, we'll win in November and end crazy Leftist policies like DEI once and for all!

Thanks again and God bless,

Winsome Earle-Sears
_Lieutenant Governor of Virginia_

The liberals can't stand it, Peter!

They simply cannot fathom that I, Winsome Earle-Sears, am a proud conservative Republican - that I dare to think **differently!**

**And they may just completely lose it when this black, female conservative becomes the next Governor of Virginia!**

_But we need your help to get there, Peter!_

_**[Will you join me today and chip in so I can win this race and continue shattering the Left's narratives?](http://links.freedomtrainalert.com/u/click?_t=2d4f229f156142619b4e4f956d2d65c9&_m=b9f0a5858b3d4be5b584171ecb6f79f2&_e=03POQ4FDn_SSQRZAxQsLcl7wW2-d7jxKkuBcmfWUUysDi7MrBv1A3hpCEhItXiks2U6ijQiewYF6NMbWKc9JmMUvxrqgjtTT9B7LEHZ-SCAxv39a8HM3XmYEv-Z2s_YYQwNlJ8_O8fDdeLAlKakwFr1qVuZbKLEovTdllpBuRW8peXS9hicPXDXfaBeX2HbhScgGkYeWCnvzL4wRhdstXmxiH0OIU2m5f3oqWUOWtgoFjLkxygiEQC3gjN4tz4_e8avlz6YMjRoqgEeuQE09P7Oe5RsbTxgUws1H5yq9HcvAF1-rFOezfUC4cqUJEn2JxxVnpVgnMzrZtu_JQGYBqzkhSmrfsBSuslzZSFyp4ChQQFYyinZ6_aYDTRgIQEGEyQkf81UAoSFTqwDMpkkIZ8cKB7owoT5rIKybf5SL_tk%3D)**_

clean_body: I won’t let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood. | | | I won’t let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood. | | | | Slaves did not die in the fields so that we could call ourselves victims now in 2025. If they saw the DEI nonsense today they would say to us, 'Is that what we died for?? NO! We died for you to have an opportunity - take it!' My father came to this country with nothing more than $1.75 in his pocket. He had next to nothing, but with education, grit, and determination, my father built a life for me and my family here. He never asked for a hand out & he never needed one! Peter - Democrats think minorities can't succeed without DEI. They think that we need their help, their labels, and their permission. But I 'm the Lt. Governor of Virginia--the former capital of the Confederacy. I didn't get where I am today through victimhood but through faith, education, and grit. Right now, I'm running to become the Governor of Virginia. My Democrat opponent, Abigail Spanberger, spews DEI nonsense at every chance she gets. But I believe in the American Dream; anyone who takes responsibility & works hard can succeed in this country! Seriously, my father is a perfect example of this; he didn't take a single handout to find success in America! DEI is a cancer.I won 't let Democrats insult the sacrifice & hard work of my father and every other minority American with their DEI handouts & victimhood. Here 's my call to action, Peter: I am really just so grateful to have you on my team in this fight. Together, we'll win in November and end crazy Leftist policies like DEI once and for all! Thanks again and God bless, Winsome Earle-Sears Lieutenant Governor of Virginia | The liberals can't stand it, Peter! **[Get the sticker](https://an.workingfamilies.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqugw92OTn3eBVs4NDg9Yvc0k4WBJ-TLjEbwMI7cs12ejbi2yhiScFmvrYnQIY0Q__Ca5gu7sny-2G9zN8OVO579d5euDCjMt_S9OQv8F8xrcsWn-wO8-tAQEEbCMtKXv6Y77OOjcS-e9wEhdAkLlVachxvvUQzI9WlxxIHos7BO1Is3mOVcdINWtmJw_ptSqqUsdev1RGy9otQJYWObzw3Tq6fBtQkW2iFWDE2_yKI1qmWquOplf7na7c8HZsoOExdTt0TmQHfT7fzw7TgwiIlrCuny0ZJO9AU3oGj_pzf4XXKJ-m8Bw2JJNAZRrd9GRK48oSiYkTqg4JtjgP3APhou/4fi/-6TfPTuTQjGyuslDZD4obQ/h2/h001.MA8TELxJ545C-t-7mbsJSpF3MqOsgU_Yb9hP2Ii89fM)** There are only about 800 billionaires in the United States right now. **But together, they have a combined wealth of over $6 trillion — more than the _half_ of the rest of the population.1** Now, billionaires like Elon Musk are using that wealth to try and steal even more from the rest of us, all so they can get bigger tax cuts for themselves and more impunity to do anything they want without any oversight or accountability. They're going after our health care. They want to slash Social Security. They're trying to break the entire federal government so that it can't work for the people. Last week, Elon even tried to buy a state Supreme Court election in Wisconsin by spending $25 million. But we fought back and handed him a landslide defeat. clean_body: Our NEW “No One Likes Billionaires” sticker just dropped, and we want to send one to you! | | | Peter, We wanted you to be the first to know — our NEW “No One Likes Billionaires” sticker just dropped, and we want to send one to you! There are only about 800 billionaires in the United States right now. But together, they have a combined wealth of over $6 trillion — more than the half of the rest of the population.1 Now, billionaires like Elon Musk are using that wealth to try and steal even more from the rest of us, all so they can get bigger tax cuts for themselves and more impunity to do anything they want without any oversight or accountability. They're going after our health care. They want to slash Social Security. They're trying to break the entire federal government so that it can't work for the people. Last week, Elon even tried to buy a state Supreme Court election in Wisconsin by spending $25 million. But we fought back and handed him a landslide defeat. Here's the thing about billionaires like Elon Musk, Peter: They may be able to buy mansions, and yachts, and private jets, and even presidencies. But there’s one thing that as hard as they try, they can’t buy: our respect. In solidarity, Team WFP Source: 1\. , USA Today , July 19, 2024 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. ## Keith Ellison caught on tape!
The Minnesota Attorney General, Keith Ellison, was captured on a December 2021 audio recording while meeting privately with a group of constituents who would later be indicted in the largest Covid-related fraud in America. ## DHS to Fine Deportation-Ordered Aliens $998 Per Day Overstayed
Illegal immigrants overstaying their deportation day will have to pay a fine of $998 per day after their official removal date. The Trump administration is enforcing a 1996 Clinton administration-era law. Though on the books, the law was enforced for the first time in 2018 during President Donald Trump’s first term, Reuters reported. clean_body: This November, the DLCC anticipates some of the toughest battlegrounds nationwide to emerge in Virginia. Earlier this week our political team announced our first four target races, take a look: | | | | | ---|--- In Virginia, we’re already preparing for one of the highest-profile elections of 2025. We’ve identified some of the most competitive districts in the commonwealth – four must-win target races where the stakes are sky-high. It’s in districts like these that we’ll fight to defend our majority in the House and secure a Democratic trifecta. | District | Candidate | 2023 Margins ---|---|--- HD-21 | Del. Josh Thomas | 3.6% HD-65 | Del. Joshua Cole | 6.0% HD-84 | Del. Nadarius Clark | 6.2% HD-97 | Del. Michael Feggans | 4.8% In the face of Donald Trump and the GOP’s overwhelming attempts to subvert our democracy and our freedoms, state legislative races are the most immediate opportunity for Democrats to defend and build sustainable power. The DLCC’s decades-long work has fostered an electoral environment for a Democratic trifecta in Virginia to finally emerge, which would shield the commonwealth from the chaos of the Trump administration and protect hard-fought progress. If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | | Thank you, Team DLCC | Prepared and paid for by DLCC Victory Fund, 1225 Eye Street NW Suite 1250, Washington, DC 20005. | | Peter, it’s time to bring the fight. I know I won’t stand by and watch as rights and freedoms are stripped from my fellow Pennsylvanians. I won’t stand by and watch as oppressive tariffs hurt our economy and raise costs of living. I won’t pretend things are okay when they’re not. **We need to know what it means to fight, because we’re going to have to fight hard in the face of Trump’s administration. ** To fight means to speak truth to power when leadership is failing to protect the people, and standing up to unelected billionaires who aim to cut Social Security and Medicaid. It means working every day to lift working families out of poverty by advocating for policies that benefit everyday Pennsylvanians, not just the ultra-rich. It means investing in public education against budget cuts and threats to dissolve the DoEd, because our kids and grandkids shouldn’t have to lose access to quality education because of a reckless federal government. **To fight means to not lose sight of what matters – _the people._** People like my working-class parents, a hairdresser and a union bus driver, who juggled careers and raising our family. Who work as hard as they’re able and still are forced to struggle. [**Will you join me in bringing the fight? We need you on our team if we’re going to send the message that Pennsylvanians are fired up »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/105510196/538575551/-1107115010?amount=10&source=sbd_ad_em_fr_040925_tofight&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MDI5M2RmYi03ODE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=j4agnrq9vHcNxTFCnOzTEbA_PASK9_qMJtcVpw3JoNg=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

PA Lieutenant Governor While serving as Lieutenant Governor, Austin Davis helped advance some of the most progressive changes the Commonwealth has seen in decades.

Now, he's helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. clean_body: Austin Davis is helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, it’s time to bring the fight. I know I won’t stand by and watch as rights and freedoms are stripped from my fellow Pennsylvanians. I won’t stand by and watch as oppressive tariffs hurt our economy and raise costs of living. I won’t pretend things are okay when they’re not. We need to know what it means to fight, because we’re going to have to fight hard in the face of Trump’s administration. To fight means to speak truth to power when leadership is failing to protect the people, and standing up to unelected billionaires who aim to cut Social Security and Medicaid. It means working every day to lift working families out of poverty by advocating for policies that benefit everyday Pennsylvanians, not just the ultra-rich. It means investing in public education against budget cuts and threats to dissolve the DoEd, because our kids and grandkids shouldn’t have to lose access to quality education because of a reckless federal government. To fight means to not lose sight of what matters – the people. People like my working-class parents, a hairdresser and a union bus driver, who juggled careers and raising our family. name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: A Clear and Present Danger
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501289079%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bA%2bClear%2band%2bPresent%2bDanger%250115423.47664705.7108705.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501289079%2501Senate%2bUpdate_%2bA%2bClear%2band%2bPresent%2bDanger%250115423.47664705.7108705.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501Friend%2501)
---|---
| | | **April 9, 2025** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
### Senate Update: A Clear and Present Danger
I've said it before, and I'll say it again: our country is facing a clear and present danger from a lawless president. This is a time for all patriots to stand up and be counted. We must not give in. We must not give up. And we must work around the clock. That's why, for two nights last week, Senate Democrats held the floor overnight. On Friday, we pushed for amendments as we worked to stop Republicans from gutting Medicaid to pay for billionaire tax giveaways. A few days before that, Senator Cory Booker held the floor by speaking for over 25 hours--and if he'd wanted to go over all of Trump's outrageous attacks on our families, it would have taken him 25 more. I was proud to [support Senator Booker's record-breaking speech by joining him on the floor with questions](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3daGN4tNty8Y8%26t%3d0s&redir_log=103852741522349); these bad times demand that we make good trouble.

For so many families, these debates have life-and-death stakes. The American people deserve a government that works for them--not one rigged against them in favor of the richest and the most powerful. So we must fight all day and all night long: in Congress, the courts, and our communities.

Here's the latest:
## Fight in the Congress
### The Great Betrayal Arrives in the Senate
Trump and Republicans are laying the groundwork for a $7 trillion bill aimed mostly at cutting taxes for the wealthy and mega corporations. How are they planning to "pay" for it? They would put much of it on the national credit card, spiking the national debt. And they would offset part of it by slashing Medicaid and public services that benefit all Americans.

We are fighting to expose this betrayal of the American people.

Remember, [we've seen this movie before](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fV5vIH839sn8&redir_log=422555753666282). During his first term, Trump promised that the benefits of tax cuts for the rich would trickle down to the rest of Americans. It never happened. He promised workers would get $4,000 pay raises. That also didn't happen--but CEOs got bigger bonuses. He promised corporations would invest their tax savings in new plants and equipment. Didn't happen. Corporations instead used much of it to buy back their own stock and jack up their share prices.

Last week, [I spoke on the Senate floor](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dg4dAc7ruR4A&redir_log=710336930975290) about the findings of a blockbuster investigation into who Trump's tax cuts benefit the most. My colleague Senator Wyden found that Trump's first round of tax cuts in 2017 helped big pharmaceutical corporations use overseas tax schemes to avoid taxes.

Now, they're doing it all again but on steroids.

That's why all through the night on Friday, Democrats offered 25 amendments to reveal who the Republicans are really working for. They rejected our amendments to protect Medicaid and Social Security, to provide tax cuts to the middle class instead of billionaires, and to protect vital programs like Meals on Wheels and child care. [We gave them every opportunity](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2foODhvQJvCNU&redir_log=904623542705290) to protect these and other priorities. They voted "NO" every time, because they don't work for American families--they sided with the billionaires and their corporate donors.

To partially pay for their tax scheme, Trump and the Republicans want to cut $880 billion from Medicaid and the Children's Health Insurance Program, which cover nearly 80 million Americans. In Maryland, Medicaid covers 1 in 3 children, 5 in 8 nursing home residents, and 1 in 3 people with disabilities.
### Trump's New National Sales Tax
This isn't the only way Donald Trump and Republicans are raising costs for American families. This week, [I also took to the floor](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3ds58F8_lGYOw&redir_log=856985522931291) to support legislation that would stop Trump from issuing his bogus emergency tariffs on Canada. His across-the-board tariffs on Canada and all other countries are, in effect, a gigantic national sales tax on the American people. When you levy a tariff on imports from Canada or Mexico, the other country doesn't pay it--American consumers do.

While targeted tariffs in strategic sectors can be an effective tool, Trump's blanket tariffs are the opposite--causing economic pain and financial chaos. Trump's tariffs have already increased prices and tanked the stock market. With his April 2 so-called "Liberation Day," he doubled down, making the turmoil even worse. Just one example: we supplement U.S. building supplies by importing Canadian lumber and other raw materials to build houses--putting tariffs on those products could increase the cost of a new home by up to $10,000. And that's not the only place it will hit American families--new cars are likely to cost thousands of dollars more.

Today, after facing widespread backlash, the Administration announced a 90-day pause on all [the newly announced "reciprocal" tariffs except for those on China, also leaving in place a 10% tariff on most countries and the earlier higher tariffs on goods like steel, autos, and auto parts](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.axios.com%2f2025%2f04%2f09%2ftrump-tariffs-90-day-pause-china&redir_log=197991684327433). This is just another example of the Trump chaos that is wreaking havoc on our economy and doing nothing to lower prices for American families.
### Standing Up for Federal Workers & the Services They Provide
In Congress, we're also continuing the fight to protect our federal workforce and the public services they provide. Senate Republicans--who control all the committees by virtue of holding the majority--are failing to uphold their constitutional duties and have refused to conduct hearings into the Trump/Musk lawbreaking spree. As a result, Democrats are holding unofficial "shadow hearings" to expose Trump and Musk's operation for what it is. In one of these hearings, [we revealed Elon Musk's dangerous lie about Ebola](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2f18P-oU2XG3E&redir_log=442579930018276): Musk said he accidentally canceled but then quickly reinstated USAID's Ebola prevention efforts, but, in fact, the lifesaving program has not resumed. We have also conducted shadow hearings on the mistreatment of our veterans, the sabotage of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, and the attacks on the NIH and medical research.

Last week, [I reintroduced legislation](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dZbBwRDwsekE&redir_log=403573790637299) to stop the Trump Administration from using yet another device to sabotage the federal government: abruptly moving agencies thousands of miles from their current locations. This endangers their critical missions, wastes taxpayer dollars, and forces workers to either lose their jobs or relocate their families. That's why I worked with Congressman Suhas Subramanyam to reintroduce the COST of Relocations Act, which requires that any federal agency preparing to relocate a significant portion of their workforce conduct and release to the public a comprehensive cost-benefit analysis of the proposed move before it is carried out. If this was really about government efficiency, Republicans would support this legislation. Somehow, I doubt they will.
## Fight in the Courts
### Protecting Vital Federal Services
Every day, Trump breaks new laws in his quest to slash vital services Americans rely on. Every day, he seeks to implement illegal orders that strip away constitutional rights, from due process to the First Amendment. And every day, I am working with lawyers to fight back in the courts. It's hard to keep track of it all--here are just a few recent updates:

One illegal, dangerous outrage that recently came to light was Trump senior officials' texting of classified war plans in an unsecured group chat, putting American troops in danger. They were so reckless that they accidentally added a journalist to the chat, who blew the whistle on this unacceptable breach of national security. As a result, I've said that [Pete Hegseth and Michael Waltz must resign](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fw6biwGXnQdE&redir_log=895032389665292) and demanded that Attorney General Bondi appoint a special counsel to investigate. Following a lawsuit filed to demand accountability, a judge ordered Hegseth and others to preserve all Signal communications from March 11-15, preventing them from disposing of the evidence. This is a vital first step.

On March 28, a judge issued a preliminary injunction barring Trump and Musk's illegal attempt to dismantle the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB was created by Congress to protect Americans from fraud and abuse by financial institutions, and it has returned $21 billion to consumers since its inception. Just last December, it returned $1.8 billion to Americans from illegal fees charged by credit repair organizations, including $42 million for Marylanders. By reclaiming that much money, the CFPB made powerful enemies. It's pretty twisted that the world's richest man is trying to "delete" an agency that helps consumers recover their hard-earned money taken by fraudsters. We're fighting to put a stop to this.

Last week, another judge temporarily blocked the Administration from cancelling $11 billion in grants that Congress passed to improve public health. The termination of these grants would undermine vital health initiatives, like vaccine distribution and substance abuse treatment in our state and local public health departments.

Many of Trump's legal defeats have come at the hands of our federal employee unions like the American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE) and the National Treasury Employees Union (NTEU). Naturally, he's now coming after these unions harder than ever - trying to strip one million federal employees of their collective bargaining rights. Using the baseless claim of threats to national security, Trump is attempting to cancel collective bargaining agreements everywhere, from the Environmental Protection Agency to the Department of the Treasury. Federal employees' collective bargaining rights are already limited - for example, they do not cover wages or benefits - and most often are in place to support whistleblower rights and other important protections. [We're fighting this illegal assault tooth and nail](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fFPuP49vOFas&redir_log=476168857148288). AFGE and NTEU have already filed suit, and last week, I led the entire Senate Democratic Caucus in a [letter](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-leads-senate-democratic-caucus-in-letter-demanding-trump-rescind-illegal-executive-order-threatening-federal-employee-collective-bargaining-agreements&redir_log=768971805131513) demanding that President Trump rescind this illegal executive order.

The legal landscape is fast-changing and complex. While we had made headway in multiple court rulings in favor of reinstating probationary employees, yesterday and today, we have faced bad news in decisions handed down by the [Supreme Court](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.washingtonpost.com%2fpolitics%2f2025%2f04%2f08%2fsupreme-court-halts-rehiring-probationary-federal-workers%2f&redir_log=290787511467759) and a panel on the [4th Circuit Court of Appeals](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.npr.org%2f2025%2f04%2f09%2fnx-s1-5357415%2ftrump-federal-probationary-employees-firings&redir_log=723297898041609). These rulings are undeniably major setbacks; however, since they were made on procedural grounds and not based on the merits, they do not prevent the possibility of future lawsuits. Meanwhile, the Supreme Court also ruled in Trump's favor today to temporarily pause the reinstatement of a member of the [Merit Systems Protection Board](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.usatoday.com%2fstory%2fnews%2fpolitics%2f2025%2f04%2f09%2fdonald-trump-supreme-court-nlrb-democrats%2f83012169007%2f&redir_log=439712553984719), hamstringing the Board from taking action for now. These decisions are another roadblock in our fight, but I'm reaching out to our partners in the legal community to formulate our next steps in this legal battle.

Trump has grown frustrated with federal judges standing up for constitutional rights and the rule of law, so now he's taken to trying to strong-arm the legal system into silence, [from bullying law firms](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fDCvJ8XMQ3Ic&redir_log=744584383921282) to calling for the impeachment of judges. That's why he and his MAGA cronies in Congress are openly threatening judges who rule against him. On March 26, House [Republicans filed articles of impeachment against a Maryland judge for doing his job](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fGSiJdu5N-w0&redir_log=058765868481285). We won't have it. Now is the time for all patriots to stand up and push back in solidarity.
### Defending our Civil Liberties
Our nation strives to uphold a few simple, powerful principles: everyone is endowed with the same natural rights and deserves the same fundamental freedoms, from freedom of speech and freedom of religion to due process under the law. No one should be afraid to express their views; everyone gets their day in court.

Every day, Donald Trump is desecrating those foundational principles. He's using an arcane piece of legislation called the Alien Enemies Act and claiming other dubious powers he does not have to trample the rights of immigrants and visiting students. Right now, [hooded agents are abducting legal residents of this country](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fl5lVdK7mmRE&redir_log=189384311571289) and disappearing them to detention centers and foreign prisons.

One of these reprehensible attacks happened in Maryland. Last Monday, the Trump Administration admitted that they had accidentally deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a father and a legal resident of Prince George's County who had legally mandated protected status. Mr. Abrego Garcia is now detained in a Salvadoran prison, and the Administration is claiming that they have no ability to bring him back. That's outrageous. Senator Alsobrooks and I demanded that the Administration take immediate action to right this wrong. On Friday, a federal judge agreed, demanding that the Trump Administration bring him home by midnight Monday. Then, Monday, the Supreme Court temporarily paused the deadline while they review the case. Let's be clear: Kilmar Abrego Garcia should've never been deported in the first place. We will fight to get him home. [Yesterday, I led a letter to the Administration, signed by 24 of my Senate Democratic colleagues, pressing for answers as to how this happened and demanding his immediate return](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-leads-24-colleagues-in-letter-demanding-answers-return-of-maryland-father-wrongfully-deported-to-el-salvador&redir_log=844824760561209). And [today I joined Mr. Abrego Garcia's family](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fyoutu.be%2fHv45k6JMyjs&redir_log=484827879486189) in a private meeting and to publicly call for his return.

On March 25, the Administration also arrested and detained Rumeysa Ozturk, a student visa holder whose only crime was writing an op-ed in the Tufts University student newspaper. Trump can disagree with what she wrote, but he cannot infringe on her right to express her views. The same day, a judge issued an order requiring the Department of Homeland Security to provide 48-hour notice before moving her from the District of Massachusetts. Flouting this order, the Administration moved her to Louisiana. [I joined Senator Warren and other colleagues to demand that Rumeysa receive the due process she deserves](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.warren.senate.gov%2fimo%2fmedia%2fdoc%2fwarren_letter_re_ozturk.pdf&redir_log=220366309865507). Supreme Court precedent is clear: individuals legally in the U.S. have First Amendment protection. The case has now been moved to a federal district court in Vermont. This is one of many cases involving the First Amendment rights of students and the Administration's efforts to punish colleges and universities.

Trump isn't just targeting individuals--he's also trying to deport whole classes of individuals who are here legally. The Trump Department of Homeland Security is trying to strip legal protected status from 350,000 Venezuelans taking refuge in the United States after fleeing danger and persecution. Fortunately, on March 31, a federal judge temporarily blocked the Trump Administration from ending these humanitarian protections. This will give the plaintiffs time to proceed with a legal challenge. On April 7, the Supreme Court ruled on procedural grounds to allow the Trump Administration to use the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelan migrants for now, because the legal challenge should have been filed in Texas and not DC. In doing so, the Court at least required that every migrant receive a court hearing before being removed. And in positive news following the Supreme Court decision, on Wednesday a federal court in Texas temporarily blocked the Administration from removals under the Alien Enemies Act, in order for the petitioners' claims against their removal to be heard fully by the court.

On Tuesday night, we had another victory for the First Amendment: a federal judge ruled that the White House must restore media access to the Associated Press. It was unlawful, the judge ruled, for the White House to cut journalists out of their pool for refusing to refer to the Gulf of Mexico as the "Gulf of America." This Administration's attacks on freedom of the press are straight out of the playbook of autocrats like Putin. Limiting fair access to the press is also an assault on Americans' access to fair and unbiased information. We need a press that covers the president without fear or favor--this ruling helps protect that principle.

We will continue to fight back in the Courts, in Congress, and, as discussed next, in our communities.
## Fight in Our Communities
[Over the weekend, I spoke at the large "Hands Off" protest in front of City Hall in Baltimore](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fyoutu.be%2fxJJM-qh4vzk&redir_log=59932501603192). It was part of a wave of demonstrations taking place all over America. I'm glad to see patriots mobilizing. Together, we are making ripples that will turn into waves. Donald Trump is coming after services Americans rely on, he's coming after members of our communities, and he's coming after our values. That's why I'm working every day to get out into our communities and join in mobilizing the American people against Trump's attacks.

Last week, we also made waves in other ways. I attended a town hall with the AFGE union members from FEMA, who rush into danger to save lives and help Americans recover from natural disasters. Trump is trying to eliminate their collective bargaining agreement, fire FEMA workers who only want to help Americans, and eliminate FEMA's role in responding to natural disasters. This is outrageous, and it's only one of the ways that Trump is going after first responders. FEMA workers risk their lives to serve their fellow Americans; they deserve so much better than being stabbed in the back. Last week, we also pushed back against a reported plan to shut down one of [FEMA's lifesaving disaster preparedness programs](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fshorts%2fPrPWAzcolAE&redir_log=100154054281292). If these programs aren't in place, Americans will die.

I also recently joined a virtual town hall with Maryland Nonprofits, a coalition that represents hundreds of organizations in our state that are at risk of losing vital federal funding to help vulnerable Marylanders with housing, health care, education, substance abuse treatment, and more. I will fight to get back every penny our families need.

Also, on Monday, I joined a meeting of the Caucus of African American Leaders of Anne Arundel County to discuss Donald Trump's attacks on our communities--including his efforts to rewrite and whitewash our history. We learned early this week that the Administration has pushed the National Park Service to downplay slavery and the role of Harriet Tubman in the story of the Underground Railroad. [After my pushback, the National Park Service backtracked](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.washingtonpost.com%2fnation%2f2025%2f04%2f08%2fnational-park-service-underground-railroad-harriet-tubman%2f&redir_log=032007395754742).

Yesterday, [I joined a coalition of postal supervisors and postal workers to fight back against Elon Musk's call for privatizing the United States Postal Service](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fwatch%3fv%3dW-f2AGarPAo&redir_log=31965141636290). Our mail is not for sale. And [today, I joined the disability community, including Marylanders with disabilities, to rally against Donald Trump and Republicans' attacks on Medicaid](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15423.47664705.7108705.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fyoutu.be%2fvmXVjgsh9bc&redir_log=985968877228195). These actions will hurt Maryland families, and especially those with disabilities, and we cannot sit quietly by as Republicans attempt to ram them through**.**
### We're In This Together
Thank you for being in this fight. The risks to our country are greater and the stakes are higher than at any other moment in my lifetime. I've been heartened to see so many Marylanders calling my office, sending me letters, attending our town halls, and rallying in the streets to defend the country they love.

We all see the same thing: a clear and present danger to our democracy, the rule of law, freedom, and the future of our country. This is a time for every patriot to stand up and fight back. Chris Van Hollen
---

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | April 9, 2025 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Senate Update: A Clear and Present Danger I've said it before, and I'll say it again: our country is facing a clear and present danger from a lawless president. This is a time for all patriots to stand up and be counted. We must not give in. We must not give up. And we must work around the clock. That's why, for two nights last week, Senate Democrats held the floor overnight. On Friday, we pushed for amendments as we worked to stop Republicans from gutting Medicaid to pay for billionaire tax giveaways. A few days before that, Senator Cory Booker held the floor by speaking for over 25 hours--and if he'd wanted to go over all of Trump's outrageous attacks on our families, it would have taken him 25 more. I was proud to ; these bad times demand that we make good trouble. For so many families, these debates have life-and-death stakes. The American people deserve a government that works for them--not one rigged against them in favor of the richest and the most powerful. So we must fight all day and all night long: in Congress, the courts, and our communities. Here's the latest: Fight in the Congress The Great Betrayal Arrives in the Senate Trump and Republicans are laying the groundwork for a $7 trillion bill aimed mostly at cutting taxes for the wealthy and mega corporations. How are they planning to "pay" for it? They would put much of it on the national credit card, spiking the national debt. And they would offset part of it by slashing Medicaid and public services that benefit all Americans. We are fighting to expose this betrayal of the American people. Remember, . During his first term, Trump promised that the benefits of tax cuts for the rich would trickle down to the rest of Americans. It never happened. He promised workers would get $4,000 pay raises. That also didn't happen--but CEOs got bigger bonuses. He promised corporations would invest their tax savings in new plants and equipment. Didn't happen. Corporations instead used much of it to buy back their own stock and jack up their share prices. Last week, about the findings of a blockbuster investigation into who Trump's tax cuts benefit the most. My colleague Senator Wyden found that Trump's first round of tax cuts in 2017 helped big pharmaceutical corporations use overseas tax schemes to avoid taxes. Now, they're doing it all again but on steroids. That's why all through the night on Friday, Democrats offered 25 amendments to reveal who the Republicans are really working for. They rejected our amendments to protect Medicaid and Social Security, to provide tax cuts to the middle class instead of billionaires, and to protect vital programs like Meals on Wheels and child care. to protect these and other priorities. They voted "NO" every time, because they don't work for American families--they sided with the billionaires and their corporate donors. To partially pay for their tax scheme, Trump and the Republicans want to cut $880 billion from Medicaid and the Children's Health Insurance Program, which cover nearly 80 million Americans. In Maryland, Medicaid covers 1 in 3 children, 5 in 8 nursing home residents, and 1 in 3 people with disabilities. Trump's New National Sales Tax This isn't the only way Donald Trump and Republicans are raising costs for American families. This week, to support legislation that would stop Trump from issuing his bogus emergency tariffs on Canada. His across-the-board tariffs on Canada and all other countries are, in effect, a gigantic national sales tax on the American people. When you levy a tariff on imports from Canada or Mexico, the other country doesn't pay it--American consumers do. While targeted tariffs in strategic sectors can be an effective tool, Trump's blanket tariffs are the opposite--causing economic pain and financial chaos. Trump's tariffs have already increased prices and tanked the stock market. With his April 2 so-called "Liberation Day," he doubled down, making the turmoil even worse. Just one example: we supplement U.S. building supplies by importing Canadian lumber and other raw materials to build houses--putting tariffs on those products could increase the cost of a new home by up to $10,000. And that's not the only place it will hit American families--new cars are likely to cost thousands of dollars more. Today, after facing widespread backlash, the Administration announced a 90-day pause on all . This is just another example of the Trump chaos that is wreaking havoc on our economy and doing nothing to lower prices for American families. Standing Up for Federal Workers & the Services They Provide In Congress, we're also continuing the fight to protect our federal workforce and the public services they provide. Senate Republicans--who control all the committees by virtue of holding the majority--are failing to uphold their constitutional duties and have refused to conduct hearings into the Trump/Musk lawbreaking spree. As a result, Democrats are holding unofficial "shadow hearings" to expose Trump and Musk's operation for what it is. In one of these hearings, : Musk said he accidentally canceled but then quickly reinstated USAID's Ebola prevention efforts, but, in fact, the lifesaving program has not resumed. We have also conducted shadow hearings on the mistreatment of our veterans, the sabotage of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, and the attacks on the NIH and medical research. Last week, to stop the Trump Administration from using yet another device to sabotage the federal government: abruptly moving agencies thousands of miles from their current locations. This endangers their critical missions, wastes taxpayer dollars, and forces workers to either lose their jobs or relocate their families. That's why I worked with Congressman Suhas Subramanyam to reintroduce the COST of Relocations Act, which requires that any federal agency preparing to relocate a significant portion of their workforce conduct and release to the public a comprehensive cost-benefit analysis of the proposed move before it is carried out. If this was really about government efficiency, Republicans would support this legislation. Somehow, I doubt they will. Fight in the Courts Protecting Vital Federal Services Every day, Trump breaks new laws in his quest to slash vital services Americans rely on. Every day, he seeks to implement illegal orders that strip away constitutional rights, from due process to the First Amendment. And every day, I am working with lawyers to fight back in the courts. It's hard to keep track of it all--here are just a few recent updates: One illegal, dangerous outrage that recently came to light was Trump senior officials' texting of classified war plans in an unsecured group chat, putting American troops in danger. They were so reckless that they accidentally added a journalist to the chat, who blew the whistle on this unacceptable breach of national security. As a result, I've said that and demanded that Attorney General Bondi appoint a special counsel to investigate. Following a lawsuit filed to demand accountability, a judge ordered Hegseth and others to preserve all Signal communications from March 11-15, preventing them from disposing of the evidence. This is a vital first step. On March 28, a judge issued a preliminary injunction barring Trump and Musk's illegal attempt to dismantle the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB was created by Congress to protect Americans from fraud and abuse by financial institutions, and it has returned $21 billion to consumers since its inception. Just last December, it returned $1.8 billion to Americans from illegal fees charged by credit repair organizations, including $42 million for Marylanders. By reclaiming that much money, the CFPB made powerful enemies. It's pretty twisted that the world's richest man is trying to "delete" an agency that helps consumers recover their hard-earned money taken by fraudsters. We're fighting to put a stop to this. Last week, another judge temporarily blocked the Administration from cancelling $11 billion in grants that Congress passed to improve public health. The termination of these grants would undermine vital health initiatives, like vaccine distribution and substance abuse treatment in our state and local public health departments. Many of Trump's legal defeats have come at the hands of our federal employee unions like the American Federation of Government Employees (AFGE) and the National Treasury Employees Union (NTEU). Naturally, he's now coming after these unions harder than ever - trying to strip one million federal employees of their collective bargaining rights. Using the baseless claim of threats to national security, Trump is attempting to cancel collective bargaining agreements everywhere, from the Environmental Protection Agency to the Department of the Treasury. Federal employees' collective bargaining rights are already limited - for example, they do not cover wages or benefits - and most often are in place to support whistleblower rights and other important protections. . AFGE and NTEU have already filed suit, and last week, I led the entire Senate Democratic Caucus in a demanding that President Trump rescind this illegal executive order. The legal landscape is fast-changing and complex. While we had made headway in multiple court rulings in favor of reinstating probationary employees, yesterday and today, we have faced bad news in decisions handed down by the and a panel on the . These rulings are undeniably major setbacks; however, since they were made on procedural grounds and not based on the merits, they do not prevent the possibility of future lawsuits. Meanwhile, the Supreme Court also ruled in Trump's favor today to temporarily pause the reinstatement of a member of the , hamstringing the Board from taking action for now. These decisions are another roadblock in our fight, but I'm reaching out to our partners in the legal community to formulate our next steps in this legal battle. Trump has grown frustrated with federal judges standing up for constitutional rights and the rule of law, so now he's taken to trying to strong-arm the legal system into silence, to calling for the impeachment of judges. That's why he and his MAGA cronies in Congress are openly threatening judges who rule against him. On March 26, House . We won't have it. Now is the time for all patriots to stand up and push back in solidarity. Defending our Civil Liberties Our nation strives to uphold a few simple, powerful principles: everyone is endowed with the same natural rights and deserves the same fundamental freedoms, from freedom of speech and freedom of religion to due process under the law. No one should be afraid to express their views; everyone gets their day in court. Every day, Donald Trump is desecrating those foundational principles. He's using an arcane piece of legislation called the Alien Enemies Act and claiming other dubious powers he does not have to trample the rights of immigrants and visiting students. Right now, and disappearing them to detention centers and foreign prisons. One of these reprehensible attacks happened in Maryland. Last Monday, the Trump Administration admitted that they had accidentally deported Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a father and a legal resident of Prince George's County who had legally mandated protected status. Mr. Abrego Garcia is now detained in a Salvadoran prison, and the Administration is claiming that they have no ability to bring him back. That's outrageous. Senator Alsobrooks and I demanded that the Administration take immediate action to right this wrong. On Friday, a federal judge agreed, demanding that the Trump Administration bring him home by midnight Monday. Then, Monday, the Supreme Court temporarily paused the deadline while they review the case. Let's be clear: Kilmar Abrego Garcia should've never been deported in the first place. We will fight to get him home. . And in a private meeting and to publicly call for his return. On March 25, the Administration also arrested and detained Rumeysa Ozturk, a student visa holder whose only crime was writing an op-ed in the Tufts University student newspaper. Trump can disagree with what she wrote, but he cannot infringe on her right to express her views. The same day, a judge issued an order requiring the Department of Homeland Security to provide 48-hour notice before moving her from the District of Massachusetts. Flouting this order, the Administration moved her to Louisiana. . Supreme Court precedent is clear: individuals legally in the U.S. have First Amendment protection. The case has now been moved to a federal district court in Vermont. This is one of many cases involving the First Amendment rights of students and the Administration's efforts to punish colleges and universities. Trump isn't just targeting individuals--he's also trying to deport whole classes of individuals who are here legally. The Trump Department of Homeland Security is trying to strip legal protected status from 350,000 Venezuelans taking refuge in the United States after fleeing danger and persecution. Fortunately, on March 31, a federal judge temporarily blocked the Trump Administration from ending these humanitarian protections. name: Team Baldwin
subject: Trump’s trade war will crush Wisconsin farmers
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TAMMY BALDWIN FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/67?t=1&akid=4651%2E35263%2ECs9vGd)
Peter, Corporations and the ultra-rich may not feel the pain of Trump’s tariffs, _but Wisconsin farmers will._ The last time Trump got us into a trade war, Wisconsin was one of the hardest hit states by retaliatory tariffs. Across the United States, we lost **$27 billion** in agricultural exports. Here in Wisconsin, we have farmers with _extremely thin_ margins who live paycheck to paycheck and are now facing potential bankruptcy or the loss of their farms. These tariffs will cause real harm. Tammy knows that our farmers tie our rural communities together and keep our economy moving forward. She’s introducing legislation to give our farmers a boost and pushing back against this administration’s harmful trade policies — but she needs you on her side. **[That’s why we’re asking: Will you chip in any amount to power Tammy’s work to protect Wisconsin farmers from failed economic policies?](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/2155?c0=stb-em-apr25.O96wkV&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250409_PM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=4651%2E35263%2ECs9vGd)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split donation will go through immediately:_ | | [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.tammybaldwin.com/go/3664?c0=stb-em-apr25.YbSOLB&refcode=AU_STB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20250409_PM1_V1_S1&t=5&akid=4651%2E35263%2ECs9vGd)

body:
This hits close to home for EMILYs List |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105510908/538577869/126959580?refcode=WUEFH422_20250409_RiggsSplit_PL_v1s4&sourceid=1055928&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg4YzgwZC03YzE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RmbZP0jLxUAquCDIBr3_mjhJHjRJ7p-VlVEhr_ssIuw=) Peter,

Jefferson Griffin is STILL trying to throw out more than 65,000 valid ballots to overturn the North Carolina Supreme Court election results in his favor.

Despite _two_ recounts confirming pro-choice champion Justice Allison Riggs won, Griffin refuses to concede, and he's doing everything he can to change the results of the election in a blatantly corrupt partisan power grab.

**At EMILYs List, this hits close to home — our Communications intern, Sidney Curtis, is one of the votes Jefferson Griffin is trying to throw out!**

Griffin's campaign never properly notified Sidney or the other 65,000+ voters how or why their ballots were being challenged. Instead, these voters received a postcard from the North Carolina Republican Party instructing them to scan a QR code, which takes them to a partisan website where they have to sift through all 65,000+ of the protested votes to find their name. _This is purposeful: Griffin is sowing confusion and frustration in his baseless attempt to overturn the election._ [**We're doing everything we can to help Justice Riggs fight back – but this legal fight is costly and we need your support. Will you split a donation between Justice Riggs and EMILYs List today?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105510909/538577870/126959580?refcode=WUEFH422_20250409_RiggsSplit_PL_v1s4&sourceid=1055928&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg4YzgwZC03YzE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RmbZP0jLxUAquCDIBr3_mjhJHjRJ7p-VlVEhr_ssIuw=) | [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105510910/538577871/126959580?refcode=WUEFH422_20250409_RiggsSplit_PL_v1s4&sourceid=1055928&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg4YzgwZC03YzE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RmbZP0jLxUAquCDIBr3_mjhJHjRJ7p-VlVEhr_ssIuw=)
---
Just this week, the North Carolina Court of Appeals ruled that every single one of these votes should be recounted and verified within 15 days OR _be thrown out_ — setting a dangerous precedent for Republicans to challenge any election they don't win going forward. Justice Riggs then appealed the case to the North Carolina Supreme Court, who has temporarily blocked the Court of Appeals' ruling.

**Peter, we knew this would be a hard fight, and we're in it for the long haul.**

North Carolinians elected Justice Riggs, and we won't allow corrupt politicians to circumvent the will of the people. Now, it's our job to make sure Justice Riggs' team has the resources they need to defend her win in court.
[**It's more important now than ever that we protect free and fair elections against GOP attempts to undermine them. So please, split a donation today between Justice Riggs and EMILYs List to ensure the will of the people wins, now and always:**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105510911/538577872/126959580?refcode=WUEFH422_20250409_RiggsSplit_PL_v1s4&sourceid=1055928&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZTg4YzgwZC03YzE1LWYwMTEtOGIzZC0wMDIyNDgyYTlmYjciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=RmbZP0jLxUAquCDIBr3_mjhJHjRJ7p-VlVEhr_ssIuw=)

clean_body: This hits close to home for EMILYs List | = Peter, Jefferson Griffin is STILL trying to throw out more than 65,000 valid ballots to overturn the North Carolina Supreme Court election results in his favor. Despite two recounts confirming pro-choice champion Justice Allison Riggs won, Griffin refuses to concede, and he's doing everything he can to change the results of the election in a blatantly corrupt partisan power grab. At EMILYs List, this hits close to home — our Communications intern, Sidney Curtis, is one of the votes Jefferson Griffin is trying to throw out! Griffin's campaign never properly notified Sidney or the other 65,000+ voters how or why their ballots were being challenged. Instead, these voters received a postcard from the North Carolina Republican Party instructing them to scan a QR code, which takes them to a partisan website where they have to sift through all 65,000+ of the protested votes to find their name. This is purposeful: Griffin is sowing confusion and frustration in his baseless attempt to overturn the election. = | = Just this week, the North Carolina Court of Appeals ruled that every single one of these votes should be recounted and verified within 15 days OR be thrown out — setting a dangerous precedent for Republicans to challenge any election they don't win going forward. Justice Riggs then appealed the case to the North Carolina Supreme Court, who has temporarily blocked the Court of Appeals' ruling. Peter, we knew this would be a hard fight, and we're in it for the long haul. North Carolinians elected Justice Riggs, and we won't allow corrupt politicians to circumvent the will of the people. Now, it's our job to make sure Justice Riggs' team has the resources they need to defend her win in court. = = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, Emily Parés (she/her/hers) Interim Vice President of State and Local Campaigns, EMILYs List = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. name: Take Action,  AOTL
subject: Now is the time: Stop the SAVE Act!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
Make your voice heard today!
|
---

Peter,
Extremists in Congress are pushing an extreme anti-voter bill — the SAVE Act — that would make it harder, if not impossible, for millions of voters across the country to register to vote. **We need your help to stop it.**
The SAVE Act would force every voter to provide a passport or birth certificate every time they register or update their voter registration which would effectively end online and mail-in voter registration.
About half of American citizens **— _ nearly 150 million citizens _— **do not have a passport, and elderly Americans are one of the demographics least likely to have a passport. This would also disproportionately impact military voters, tribal communities, rural Americans, disaster survivors, people of color, and working-class Americans — silencing eligible voters and undermining our democracy.
This bill isn’t about election integrity. It’s about silencing voters. There’s _zero evidence of widespread voter fraud,_ and states already have secure systems in place to verify voter eligibility. **The real goal of the SAVE Act? To make it harder for eligible Americans to vote.**
**We can’t let them get away with this.[Click below to send an email to your representatives in Congress and demand they vote NO on the SAVE Act.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/105511821/538580748/395572353?refcode=eng_aotl_tier1_email_SAVEActNOR_email_20250409-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEActNOR_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=eng&utm_content=20250409-AOTL-Tier1-SAVEActNOR_&utm_campaign=SAVEActNOR&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk3OGMxZTM2LTdkMTUtZjAxMS04YjNkLTAwMjI0ODJhOWZiNyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=NlrpWQq86x34oYX14FdtZ7cMg1rCBPBej_S20Jh6LXU=&emci=926883f3-be14-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&emdi=978c1e36-7d15-f011-8b3d-0022482a9fb7&ceid=8431045)**

## Trump Pauses Tariffs for 90 Days, Puts China in the Crosshairs As Stocks Soar – RedState
President Donald Trump made the surprise decision on Wednesday to pause his newly instituted tariff regime and focus his energy on China. According to his statement, a 90-day pause will be instituted while various agreements are hammered out. The exception? name: Jena for Colorado
subject: Colorado needs a fighter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jena for Colorado
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250409-email [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250409-email]
Peter,
Donald Trump and his extremist allies are attacking our rights and threatening our democracy. We have to stand strong.
That’s why Jena is running for Attorney General. She’s already taken on Trump and MAGA extremists as Secretary of State—fighting to protect our elections, expand voting rights, and stand up to those who threaten our democracy. But now, just a few months into Trump’s presidency, we have seen the lengths he and his far-right allies will go to roll back our freedoms. The stakes are higher than ever.
Colorado needs an AG who will stand on the frontlines to defend our rights and democracy and always fight for working people and the middle class.
Our first week of fundraising sets the tone for this entire race. Can we count on you to join us by chipping in today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250409-email]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

This fight won’t be easy. We need grassroots supporters like you to help us build a campaign strong enough to win.
Every dollar you give helps us reach voters, spread Jena’s message, and fight for the future of Colorado.
Donate today to help us win this race and protect the values that matter most to Coloradans. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/2402-q2-jg-launch?refcode=250409-email]
Thank you for standing with us,
Jena For Colorado
